digest: Standard-depth audit finds the most defensible path is SCI-style reporting with stack-specific measurement: direct telemetry for self-hosted GPUs/hosts, provider carbon exports for cloud estates, and clearly caveated proxy estimates for managed LLM APIs.

finalReport.summary: Working conclusion: a small SaaS team should report AI inference energy/carbon as an engineering estimate, not a compliance-grade carbon inventory. Use an SCI-style boundary and functional unit; collect direct GPU/CPU/host telemetry when infrastructure is controlled; use AWS/GCP/Azure aggregate cloud carbon exports for cloud-estate context; and use token/request/runtime logs plus labeled proxies for managed GenAI APIs because inspected OpenAI, Bedrock, and Vertex/Gemini API references do not expose per-call energy/carbon fields. Avoid universal factors for grid carbon intensity, PUE, or model energy; publish assumptions and confidence.

researchMetadata.expectedFinalShape: bounded parent-facing research handoff plus claim verdict index
researchMetadata.sourcePolicy: Primary vendor, kernel, standards/specification, and maintained tool documentation preferred; negative provider-field claims are limited to inspected API references.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: DCGM/dcgm-exporter for NVIDIA GPU energy/power; Linux powercap/RAPL for CPU energy; IPMI/BMC as node-level cross-check.
AWS/GCP/Azure provide monthly estimated cloud carbon reporting at account/billing/project/service/resource dimensions, not per-inference telemetry.
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: Determines provider telemetry feasibility and allocation granularity.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: OpenAI, Bedrock, and Vertex/Gemini docs checked expose usage/invocation/token metadata rather than per-call energy/carbon; Anthropic evidence is insufficient.
factSlotCoverage.evidenceStatus: verified for inspected providers; incomplete for Anthropic/Azure OpenAI

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: Energy varies by task, modality, model architecture/size, output tokens, batching, prefill/decode behavior, KV cache, quantization, utilization, and latency/SLA.
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: Defines workload logs and allocation denominators.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: Use SCI-style carbon per functional unit: (operational energy×carbon intensity + embodied emissions) / disclosed R, with matching period and denominator.
SCI uses region-specific gCO2eq/kWh and excludes market-based instruments; GHG Scope 2 guidance governs purchased-electricity accounting.
factSlotCoverage.evidenceStatus: verified methodology; missing exact current factor dataset
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: Final numbers require source/date/geography for carbon factors.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: Include PUE/equivalent data-center efficiency when boundary includes data-center operations; avoid universal PUE constants.
Allocate hardware embodied emissions by total embodied emissions × time-share × resource-share; disclose omissions/proxies when cloud/API data unavailable.
GHG/ISO standards cover organizational/product accounting; SCI is most directly applicable to software intensity and functional-unit estimates.
CodeCarbon, Cloud Carbon Footprint, EcoLogits, Scaphandre, and experiment trackers cover different stacks; maintenance/production fit varies.
factSlotCoverage.evidenceStatus: partially supported for exact tool-fit recommendation
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: Supports practical tooling recommendations with caveats.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: Disclose boundary, functional unit, methodology, conversion factors, measured vs modeled data, allocation assumptions, embodied treatment, and confidence/uncertainty.
factSlotCoverage.evidenceStatus: partially supported: SCI supports most disclosures; uncertainty/confidence is good practice

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: Managed API: proxy/token logging; cloud estate: provider/CCF exports; self-hosted/cloud GPU: host/GPU telemetry plus workload allocation.
factSlotCoverage.evidenceStatus: partially supported for small-team stack-specific tool prescription

mainFindings.finding: Direct telemetry is strongest when the team controls inference infrastructure: NVIDIA DCGM/dcgm-exporter can expose GPU power/energy and utilization, and Linux powercap/RAPL exposes CPU package energy counters.
Major cloud carbon tools are useful for delayed aggregate cloud-emissions reporting, but not per-request AI inference telemetry.
sources: AWS Customer Carbon Footprint Tool docs

mainFindings.finding: For managed LLM APIs, inspected OpenAI, Bedrock, and Vertex/Gemini references expose usage, token, invocation, or generation metadata rather than per-call energy/carbon fields.
mainFindings.evidenceStatus: verified negative evidence for inspected references, not exhaustive provider absence

mainFindings.finding: Request count alone is a weak denominator; task, modality, model size/architecture, output length, and prefill/decode behavior materially affect inference energy.
SCI gives the cleanest reporting structure for software intensity: SCI=(O+M) per functional unit, with O=energy×carbon intensity and M for embodied emissions.
Narrow AI inference metrics should not be marketed as full GHG Protocol or ISO organizational/product compliance unless the boundary and lifecycle scope actually match those standards.

recommendations.recommendation: Adopt an SCI-style reporting template for any customer/product/request estimate: boundary, functional unit, measurement period, energy source, carbon-intensity source, PUE treatment, embodied-emissions treatment, allocation denominator, and confidence label.
recommendations.evidenceStatus: verified plus partial for uncertainty/confidence as good practice

recommendations.recommendation: If self-hosting or controlling GPU nodes, instrument DCGM/dcgm-exporter for GPU counters and collect workload logs that include model, prompt/prefill tokens, decode/output tokens, batch size, latency, cache status, and hardware-time denominator.

For managed LLM APIs, do not claim measured per-call energy/carbon unless the provider supplies such telemetry. Report token/request-based estimates as modeled proxies and label provider coverage gaps.
recommendations.evidenceStatus: verified for inspected OpenAI/Bedrock/Vertex references; partial provider coverage

recommendations.recommendation: For cloud-estate reporting, use provider carbon exports or Cloud Carbon Footprint-style tooling for aggregate emissions context, then separately allocate inference workload using measured or logged workload denominators.

Avoid hard-coded universal grid factors, PUE values, or model energy constants. Require factor source, geography, timestamp/period, and whether values are measured or modeled before publishing numbers.
recommendations.evidenceStatus: verified methodology; exact factor datasets not selected

actionPlan.step: Define reporting boundary and functional unit, e.g. per API request, per generated token, per customer-month, or per completed job; document why the unit matches the product decision.

Implement workload logging: provider/model, model version where available, prompt and output tokens, request count, batch size, cache hit/miss, latency, region, tenant/customer, and inference runtime or node/GPU time.
Choose measurement path by stack: managed API uses labeled proxy estimates; cloud estate uses provider/CCF exports; controlled GPU/host serving uses DCGM/RAPL/host telemetry with allocation logic.
Convert energy to carbon only with documented region/time carbon-intensity data and documented PUE/equivalent overhead when in boundary.

Publish results in tiers: measured, modeled from provider aggregate, modeled from tokens/requests, and excluded/unknown. Keep uncertainty visible rather than blending all estimates into one precision-looking number.
actionPlan.evidenceStatus: partial for exact tiering; supported by disclosure principles

caveatedFindings.finding: Credible AI inference reporting should disclose uncertainty/confidence, but inspected SCI sources support boundary/methodology/factor disclosure more directly than a formal uncertainty requirement.
caveatedFindings.whyItMatters: Use confidence labels as prudent transparency, not as a claimed SCI mandate.

caveatedFindings.finding: Small-SaaS tool choice by stack is supported at the category level, but exact production fit and maintenance quality require current release/activity checks.
caveatedFindings.whyItMatters: Do not overcommit to a tool without validating compatibility, maintenance, and deployment overhead.

caveatedFindings.finding: Study-specific energy examples from arXiv 2311.16863v3 are useful to show variation, but should not be used as universal production constants.

unverifiedButRelevant.claim: Prefer NVML/DCGM APIs over parsing nvidia-smi text because nvidia-smi output is not guaranteed backward compatible.
IPMI/BMC sensors can cross-check node-level power but usually cannot attribute request/process energy without allocation logic.
unverifiedButRelevant.whyItMatters: Useful validation method but not a substitute for workload allocation.

unverifiedButRelevant.claim: Anthropic API evidence was insufficient for a strong absence claim on per-call energy/carbon telemetry.
Batching improves throughput/utilization but changes latency and comparability; log batch size, queue time, scheduler mode, and SLA.
Quantization can reduce memory footprint/bandwidth pressure, but no universal energy-reduction factor was found.
CarbonTracker, Experiment Impact Tracker, and eco2AI are more experiment/local-script trackers than full production SaaS attribution systems.
MLCO2 Impact states it is passively maintained with no active data/code updates.
unverifiedButRelevant.whyItMatters: Avoid recommending stale tools without maintenance check.

unverifiedButRelevant.claim: Commercial carbon APIs such as Climatiq can convert activity data to CO2e but do not provide AI workload attribution by themselves.

parentDecisionNotes.note: Decide whether the goal is internal optimization, customer-facing disclosure, or compliance reporting.
parentDecisionNotes.whyItMatters: SCI-style estimates are suitable for software intensity and operational decisions; GHG/ISO compliance needs broader boundary and audit controls.
parentDecisionNotes.suggestedParentDecision: Use this research for engineering/product estimates unless a formal carbon inventory engagement is planned.

parentDecisionNotes.note: Choose the functional unit before collecting numbers.
parentDecisionNotes.whyItMatters: Per request, per token, per customer-month, and per job answer different questions and require different allocation denominators.
parentDecisionNotes.suggestedParentDecision: Pick one primary unit and one secondary diagnostic unit; disclose both if needed.

parentDecisionNotes.note: For managed APIs, completeness conflicts with verification strictness.
parentDecisionNotes.whyItMatters: The strongest evidence supports lack of documented per-call telemetry in inspected APIs, but Anthropic/Azure OpenAI coverage is incomplete and absence claims are inherently fragile.
parentDecisionNotes.suggestedParentDecision: Phrase as 'inspected provider APIs did not document per-call energy/carbon telemetry' rather than universal provider absence.

parentDecisionNotes.note: Do not publish exact carbon numbers until factor dataset, PUE/overhead treatment, and period/geography are selected.
parentDecisionNotes.whyItMatters: Unsupported constants would make the final estimate look more precise than the evidence allows.
parentDecisionNotes.suggestedParentDecision: Require a named factor source and date for any external-facing numeric estimate.

parentDecisionNotes.note: Tool recommendations need a current maintenance and integration pass.
parentDecisionNotes.whyItMatters: The audit verified category fit more than operational maturity or current releases.
parentDecisionNotes.suggestedParentDecision: Run a short tool spike before committing to production instrumentation.

researchScopeCoverage.hardwareProviderTelemetry: covered for NVIDIA GPU, Linux CPU/RAPL, and cloud provider carbon exports; incomplete for non-NVIDIA accelerators and TPU direct counters
researchScopeCoverage.allocationMethodology: covered via SCI operational and embodied formulas plus functional-unit allocation
researchScopeCoverage.runtimeFactors: covered for task/model/output tokens, prefill/decode, batching, utilization, and quantization caveats
researchScopeCoverage.standardsGuidance: covered for SCI, GHG Protocol, ISO 14064-1/14067 scope distinctions
researchScopeCoverage.tooling: covered by category; production maintenance ranking not fully verified
researchScopeCoverage.smallSaaSFeasibility: covered as stack-specific approach with caveats

remainingGaps.gap: Primary evidence incomplete for Anthropic and Azure OpenAI per-request energy/carbon fields.
remainingGaps.whyItMatters: Could change provider-specific advice or caveat wording.
remainingGaps.suggestedResolution: Fetch and inspect current Anthropic and Azure OpenAI API/usage/admin docs plus sustainability disclosures.

remainingGaps.gap: No exact current grid carbon-intensity dataset/factors by region/time were selected.
remainingGaps.whyItMatters: Cannot defensibly compute or recommend exact carbon values without factor provenance.
remainingGaps.suggestedResolution: Select authoritative factor sources by geography and reporting purpose before publishing numbers.

remainingGaps.gap: No provider/site-specific PUE numeric values selected.
remainingGaps.whyItMatters: Data-center overhead can materially change estimates when included in boundary.
remainingGaps.suggestedResolution: Use provider-published PUE/CCF methodology values or site-specific operations data; disclose if omitted.

remainingGaps.gap: Non-NVIDIA accelerator telemetry such as AMD, Intel accelerators, and TPU direct counters was not verified.
remainingGaps.whyItMatters: Guidance may not transfer to non-NVIDIA stacks.
remainingGaps.suggestedResolution: Run targeted research if the deployment uses these accelerators.

remainingGaps.gap: Tool maintenance ranking and production readiness were not fully checked.
remainingGaps.whyItMatters: Category-fit tools may be stale or unsuitable for a small SaaS production environment.
remainingGaps.suggestedResolution: Check releases, issues, supported platforms, and integration effort during implementation spike.

claims.support: DCGM job stats and dcgm-exporter expose GPU energy/power and utilization fields.
claims.caveat: Telemetry is general GPU/workload telemetry, not inference semantic attribution by itself.

claims.support: Linux powercap/RAPL exposes energy_uj and max range counters; no instantaneous RAPL power_uw.
claims.caveat: Delta/wraparound handling is an engineering implication.

claims.support: Major cloud carbon tools expose delayed aggregate estimated emissions, not per-request inference telemetry.
claims.caveat: Granularity differs; AWS CCFT deprecation noted for June 30, 2026.

claims.support: OpenAI usage/cost schemas list usage/cost fields but no energy/carbon fields.
claims.caveat: Negative evidence from inspected schema, not explicit absence statement.

claims.support: Bedrock and Vertex references list invocation/usage metadata without per-call energy/carbon fields; Google carbon export is aggregate.
claims.correctionOrCounterclaim: Say attribution needs estimates/proxies or aggregate exports.

claims.support: Fixed request counts still show large energy variation by task, modality, architecture, size, and output length.
Prefill/decode differ in utilization/latency; vLLM exposes separate phase metrics.
claims.caveat: Logging recommendation inferred from documented distinctions.

claims.support: SCI=(O+M) per R; O=E×I; R is disclosed functional unit.
SCI divides total component carbon over a period by functional units in same period; conversion factors required for mixed units.
claims.caveat: Spec uses SCI scoring/rate language rather than allocation wording.

claims.support: SCI defines operational emissions as energy times region-specific gCO2eq/kWh and excludes market-based instruments.
claims.caveat: Scope 2 landing page not detailed; SCI provides decisive support.

claims.support: Include PUE/equivalent efficiency when data-center operations are in boundary; CCF formula includes PUE and grid factors.
SCI gives M=TE×TS×RS and prefers granular LCA/device data.
claims.caveat: Allows estimates when granular data unavailable.

claims.support: GHG/ISO pages map standards to organizational or product/lifecycle scopes.
SCI supports most disclosure elements for boundary, functional unit, method, factors, embodied treatment, and allocation assumptions.
claims.caveat: Uncertainty/confidence disclosure is advisable but not directly required by inspected sources.
claims.correctionOrCounterclaim: Use narrower SCI disclosure claim plus good-practice uncertainty labels.

claims.support: Docs support different measurement categories for GenAI API tracking, cloud usage, and host/GPU telemetry.
claims.caveat: Exact production fit and allocation accuracy vary; EcoLogits wording should be request/SDK/API tracking rather than proxy.
claims.correctionOrCounterclaim: Replace 'token/request proxy tools such as EcoLogits' with 'request/SDK/API tracking tools such as EcoLogits'.
